title: Medu Vada

ingredients.name: urad dal (ulundhu), soaked 3–4 hours
visual_checks: soak until the dal splits and whitens; drain completely before grinding
ingredients.notes: soak until the dal splits and whitens; drain completely before grinding

subs.name: Add 2 tbsp rice flour to the urad-dal batter
subs.notes: makes the exterior extra crispy; add with the spices, not during grinding

subs.name: dried-red-chilli (ground to a coarse powder)
subs.notes: more heat but without visible green flecks

ingredients.name: oil
visual_checks: for deep frying; fill kadai to at least 8 cm depth
ingredients.notes: for deep frying; fill kadai to at least 8 cm depth

steps.text: Drain the soaked urad dal thoroughly — even a little excess water makes the batter runny. Do not rinse after draining.

Grind the urad dal in a mixie with minimal water (add 1 tbsp at a time, using 2–3 tbsp maximum) until very smooth and almost fluffy. The batter should be thick enough to stand in peaks — test by dropping a small ball in a glass of water: it should float. Grind in short pulses, scraping the sides, for 5–6 minutes.

Transfer batter to a bowl and beat vigorously with your hand or a spoon for 2 minutes to incorporate air — this is what makes vada light and airy inside. Fold in ginger, green chilli, black pepper, asafoetida, curry leaves and salt.
Heat oil in a deep kadai to 170°C (medium heat). Test by dropping a small ball of batter — it should rise to the surface in 3–4 seconds without burning immediately.

Wet both palms with water. Take a lime-sized ball of batter, flatten it on your palm into a disc about 1 cm thick, then poke a hole in the centre with a wet index finger. Gently slide it off your palm into the hot oil. Work in batches of 3–4.

Fry on medium flame, turning once, until both sides are deep golden and the vada sounds hollow when tapped — about 3–4 minutes total. Remove with a slotted spoon and drain on paper. Do not crowd the kadai.
Serve hot immediately with coconut chutney and sambar for dipping. Medu vada loses its crispiness quickly — it must be eaten within 10 minutes of frying for the best experience.

cross_contact_notes: Ingredient metadata cannot prove an allergen-free kitchen. Check packaged labels and cross-contact statements for your products.
safety_warnings: Keep water away from hot oil, lower food gently, avoid overfilling the vessel and never leave frying unattended.
trust.provenance_summary: AI-assisted original draft; declared licence original.
trust.substitution_warning: A substitution can introduce allergens or change dietary suitability. Never claim a swap is safe without checking the replacement's label and the user's allergy context.

substitution_notes: urad-dal → Add 2 tbsp rice flour to the urad-dal batter (makes the exterior extra crispy; add with the spices, not during grinding); green-chilli → dried-red-chilli (ground to a coarse powder) (more heat but without visible green flecks)
